evals.prompt: Generate Kibana release notes from this raw tool output file: evals/files/sample-minor-release.md
evals.expected_output: Produces formatted Kibana 9.4.0 release notes with both Features and enhancements and Fixes sections, correct component sub-headings, PR links using {{kib-pull}}, and a Notes section with confidence assessments.
expectations: Output contains a ## 9.4.0 header with [elastic-kibana-9.4.0-release-notes] anchor
Output contains ### Features and enhancements section before ### Fixes section
PR links use {{kib-pull}} format like [#210456]({{kib-pull}}210456)
ES|QL is normalized to {{esql}} (not written as ES|QL or ESQL)
Alerting items involving Cases use the sub-heading Alerting and cases
Fleet items appear under Data ingestion and Fleet sub-heading, not just Fleet
Lens/Dashboard items appear under Dashboards and Visualizations sub-heading
Platform items appear under Kibana platform sub-heading
Includes cross-references to Elastic Observability and Elastic Security release notes
Bullets are verb-first and present tense
Output ends with a Notes (Confidence + Assumptions) section outside the code block

evals.prompt: Generate Kibana release notes from this raw tool output file: evals/files/sample-patch-release.md
evals.expected_output: Produces formatted Kibana 9.3.2 patch release notes with only a Fixes section (no Features and enhancements heading since there are no features or enhancements).
expectations: Output contains a ## 9.3.2 header with [elastic-kibana-9.3.2-release-notes] anchor
Output contains a ### Fixes section
Output does NOT contain a ### Features and enhancements section (no feature/enhancement items exist)
PR links use {{kib-pull}} format
Platform items appear under Kibana platform sub-heading (not just Platform)
Bullets are verb-first and present tense

evals.prompt: Generate Kibana release notes from this raw tool output file: evals/files/sample-with-breaking-changes.md
evals.expected_output: Produces formatted release notes that map items into Features/Fixes sections only. Breaking changes and deprecations are NOT added as section headings but are flagged in the Notes section for routing to separate files.
expectations: Output does NOT contain a ### Breaking Changes heading in the Markdown code block
Output does NOT contain a ### Deprecations heading in the Markdown code block
Notes section flags the breaking change item (xpack.security.authProviders) for routing to breaking-changes.md
Notes section flags the deprecation item (fleet.agents.elasticsearch.host) for routing to deprecations.md
Connectors/MCP items appear under the Connectivity sub-heading
Search Playground items appear under the Search sub-heading
Output still has correctly formatted Features and enhancements and Fixes sections for the remaining items

evals.prompt: Can you summarize recent changes to the Kibana dashboard panel architecture? I'm writing a blog post.
evals.expected_output: The skill should NOT trigger for this request. This is a general question about Kibana architecture, not a request to generate release notes from raw tool output.
expectations: Does NOT produce formatted release notes Markdown in a code block
Does NOT attempt to fetch PR context or prior release notes
Does NOT generate component sub-headings or PR link formatting
Recognizes this is not a release notes generation request
